title: nasa triphylla subsp. rudis
intro: Nasa triphylla subsp. rudis, also known as Andean mint or Chilean mint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. This plant is native to the Andes mountains and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and digestive benefits. The leaves and stems of Nasa triphylla subsp. rudis can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, nausea, and headaches, as well as to soothe sore muscles and joints. Additionally, its essential oil has been studied for its potential antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various health issues.
